# On Virtues

> A comprehensive framework of virtues proposed for consensus, covering supreme virtues (Faith, Charity, Hope), cardinal virtues (Prudence, Justice, Temperance, Fortitude), and natural virtues. Explore virtue ethics, moral philosophy, and ethical frameworks.

The Laurel Project presents a systematic framework for understanding and categorizing virtues. This resource covers the philosophical foundations, hierarchies, dependencies, and practical applications of virtues across traditions.

## Core Virtues

- [1. Miracle](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/miracle): Proof beyond reason and transcendent truth — the supreme virtue of the miraculous and divine intervention.
- [3. Faith, Reason](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/reason/): Belief in concepts, rational demonstration, and the intersection of faith and logos.
- [2. Order](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/order): Provable Order and Hierarchy — organizing principles, ranking, and systematic understanding.
- [4. Economy](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/economy): Managing goodness and resources wisely — stewardship and resource allocation.
- [5. Charity](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/charity): Making efforts for the benefit of others, encouraging altruism and volunteering.
- [6. Goodness](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/goodness): Sacrifice, Courage, and Civilization — investing virtue for the benefit of others and society.
- [7. Hope](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/hope): The will to evolve, potential for growth, and capacity for invention and integration.
- [8. Harmony](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/harmony): Generosity, Humility, and Gratitude — respectful relationships and noble conduct.
- [9. Prudence](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/prudence): Practical wisdom, strategic thinking, and wise decision-making.
- [10. Forgiveness](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/forgiveness): Repentance, letting go of wrongs, and enabling reconciliation through mercy and compassion.
- [11. Justice](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/justice): Righteousness, fairness in judgment, and moral objectivity.
- [12. Governance](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/governance): Leadership, management, and organizing others toward common goals.
- [13. Temperance](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/temperance): Self-governance, moderation, and balance between hedonism and asceticism.
- [14. Fairness](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/fairness): Creating equitable conditions for competition, impartiality, and equal treatment.
- [15. Fortitude](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/fortitude): Willpower, courage, physical and mental strength, perseverance, and endurance.
- [16. Competition](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/competition): Healthy rivalry, striving for excellence, and meritocracy.
- [17. Measurability](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/measurability): Objective assessment, selection, and reality perception.
- [18. Market](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/market): Transparent exchange, trade, commerce, and economic interaction.
- [19. Abundance](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/abundance): Wealth, vital energy, productivity, and prosperity.
- [20. Industriousness](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/industriousness): Hard work, diligence, and productive effort.
- [21. Survival](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/survival): Life, health, and the fundamental virtue of existence and self-preservation.

## Framework & Theory

- [Virtues Overview](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/virtues): Complete framework of supreme, cardinal, and natural virtues with hierarchy and dependencies.
- [Principles](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/principles): Fundamental principles underlying virtues and their relationships.
- [Hierarchies](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/hierarchies): Hierarchical organization from supreme to natural virtues, dependencies, and connections.
- [Dictionary of Concepts](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/concepts): Comprehensive dictionary of ethical concepts and their relationships to virtues.
- [Reduction to 1 Concept](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/1concept): How complex virtue systems can be distilled to fundamental principles.
- [First Trinity](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/1trinity/): Truth, Beauty, Goodness.
- [Second Trinity](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/2trinity/): Continuity, Governance, Empathy.
- [Composed Virtues](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/composed/): Virtues arising from combinations of fundamental virtues.
- [Methodology](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/methodology): Methodology and limitations of the virtue ethics framework.

## Cross-Tradition & Applied

- [The 99 Names of Allah](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/allah): Islamic divine attributes mapped to the virtue framework.
- [Scenarios](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/scenarios/): Practical scenarios and case studies illustrating virtues in action.
- [Institutions](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/institutions/): Organizations and social structures that embody and foster virtuous behavior.
- [Other Systems](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/systems/): VIA Inventory, Maslow's Hierarchy, resilience factors, and developmental assets.
- [Making Virtues Computable](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/computable/): Computational approaches to encoding and measuring virtues.

## Resources

- [Bibliography](https://virtues.provable.dev/docs/bibliography): Complete bibliography and sources for the virtue ethics framework.
- [Blog](https://virtues.provable.dev/blog): Updates and articles on virtue ethics.
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/ctzurcanu/virtues): Source code and contribution guidelines.
